---
layout: layouts/soldier.njk
title: SP4 Larry Cate
slug: cate-larry
breadcrumb: SP4 Larry Cate

# ── IDENTITY ──────────────────────────────────────
first_name: Larry
last_name: Cate
nickname:
middle_name:
rank: "SP4 / Door Gunner"
mos: "Recon · 11F · Door Gunner (from late July 1971)"
platoon: "Cat (Wild Cat) Platoon"

# ── SERVICE ───────────────────────────────────────
arrived: "1970"
departed: "1971"
hometown: "Powell, Knox Co., TN"
character_of_service: Honorable
status: Deceased
year_deceased: 2011

# ── PROFILE PHOTO ─────────────────────────────────
profile_photo: larry-cate2.jpg

# ── DECORATIONS ───────────────────────────────────
decorations:
  - "Combat Infantry Badge"
  - "Vietnam Service Medal w/2 Stars"
  - "Vietnam Campaign Medal w/60 Device"
  - "National Defense Service Medal"

distinguished_decorations:
  - "Combat Infantry Badge"

# ── FAMILY CONTACT ────────────────────────────────
family_contact: false

# ── TIMELINE SOURCE NOTE ──────────────────────────
timeline_source: >
  Reconstructed from unit records, the McGrew field calendar, accounts
  from fellow soldiers, and photographs taken by SGT Marvin Dale Miller.
  Compiled by the Miller family, 2024–2025. Larry's exact arrival and
  departure dates are under ongoing research.

# ── SERVICE TIMELINE ──────────────────────────────
timeline:

  - date: "1970"
    type: move
    tags:
      - { type: m, label: Arrival }
    headline: Arrives In-Country — Cat Platoon
    body: >
      Larry Cate arrived in Vietnam and was assigned to Cat (Wild Cat) Platoon,
      Delta Company, 2nd Battalion, 8th Cavalry, 1st Cavalry Division (Airmobile).
      Exact arrival date is under research.

  - date: "20 Apr 1971"
    type: combat
    tags:
      - { type: c, label: Combat Action }
    headline: Contact North of FSB Fanning — Three KIA
    body: >
      Delta Company made contact with an NVA base camp north of Route 1,
      east of Gia Rai Mountain. Cat Platoon's patrol spotted enemy soldiers
      bathing in a creek. Range Platoon, crossing to the north bank, walked
      into a fortified bunker complex. CPL James Cardwell, CPL Danny Drinkard,
      and CPL Joseph Hall were killed. Larry was present with Cat Platoon
      during this action.
    source_notice: >
      The summary above draws from written depositions of Capt. Bill Neal
      and SSgt Stan Dillon, supplemented by accounts from other unit members.
      Details of sequence, positioning, and circumstance vary between accounts.
      No single account should be read as a definitive record.

  - date: "24 Apr 1971"
    type: combat
    tags:
      - { type: c, label: Incident }
    headline: Huey Crash at FSB Fontaine — Three Killed
    body: >
      A Huey departing FSB Fontaine crashed shortly after takeoff, killing
      the pilot, co-pilot, and Battalion Armor SP4 Richard Colburn. Larry
      was present at FSB Fontaine at the time of the crash.

  - date: "May 1971"
    type: combat
    tags:
      - { type: c, label: Combat Action }
    headline: Capture of a Chieu Hoi
    body: >
      Cat Platoon was involved in the capture of a Chieu Hoi — a Viet Cong
      or NVA soldier who surrendered under the South Vietnamese amnesty program.
      Exact date and circumstances are under research.

  - date: "Late Jul 1971"
    type: move
    tags:
      - { type: m, label: Role Change }
    headline: Transitions to Door Gunner
    body: >
      Larry moved from ground operations to a door gunner position on a
      UH-1 Huey during unit rotations in late July 1971. His Warrant Officer
      designation is under research.

  - date: "1971"
    type: move
    tags:
      - { type: m, label: Discharge }
    headline: Returns Home — Honorable Discharge
    body: >
      Larry Cate returned home to Powell, Tennessee following his tour.
      Exact departure date is under research.

# ── PHOTOS ────────────────────────────────────────
photo_intro: >
  All photographs of Larry Cate in this archive were taken by his fellow
  Cat Platoon soldier SGT Marvin Dale Miller, presumed before August 1971
  when Larry transitioned to a door gunner role.

wartime_content_notice: false

photos:
  - filename: larry-cate1.jpg
    subfolder: field
    caption: >
      Larry Cate seated on sandbags at a fire support base, Vietnam 1971.
      A belt of machine gun ammunition is visible beside him.
    caption_short: Larry Cate on sandbags — ammunition belt visible
    credit: "Photographed by SGT Marvin D. Miller · 1971"
    photographer: unknown-of

  - filename: larry-cate-w-soldier.jpg
    subfolder: field
    caption: >
      Larry Cate with an unidentified soldier in a defoliated area, Vietnam 1971.
      Location under research. If you can identify the other soldier or the location,
      please use the contribute form.
    caption_short: Larry Cate with unidentified soldier — defoliated treeline
    credit: "Photographed by SGT Marvin D. Miller · 1971"
    photographer: unknown-of
    wide: true

  - filename: ken-weaver_larry-fishell_larry-cate.jpg
    subfolder: field
    caption: >
      Left to right: Ken Weaver (Cat Plt, 1971–72), Larry Fishell (Cat Plt, 1970–71),
      and Larry Cate (Cat Plt, 1970–71). Photographed at a fire support base, 1971.
    caption_short: Ken Weaver · Larry Fishell · Larry Cate — Cat Platoon
    credit: "Photographed by SGT Marvin D. Miller · 1971"
    wide: true

# ── DOCUMENTS ─────────────────────────────────────
documents:
